steps.title: Rewrite the quadratic in standard form
steps.content: Multiply both sides of $$-x^2 + bx - 81 = 0$$ by $-1$ to get $$x^2 - bx + 81 = 0$$. Here $a=1$, the linear coefficient is $-b$, and the constant term is $c=81$.

steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition for no real solutions
steps.content: The discriminant is $$D = (-b)^2 - 4(1)(81) = b^2 - 324$$. For no real solutions we need $$D \lt 0$$, i.e. $$b^2 - 324 \lt 0$$ which simplifies to $$b^2 \lt 324$$ or $$|b| \lt 18$$. Hence $b$ must be an integer satisfying $-17 \le b \le 17$.

steps.title: Find the least possible integer b
steps.content: From the inequality $$|b| \lt 18$$ the smallest integer value is $-17$. Therefore the least possible $b$ is $-17$. The answer is -17.
